name: Aborting and uninstalling Ollama downloads
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

The user needed help aborting a partially downloaded Ollama model (phi4-mini-reasoning) and cleaning up the incomplete files. They had started downloading the model but interrupted it with Ctrl+C when it was 41% complete (1.3GB of 3.2GB downloaded). When they tried to remove the model using `ollama rm phi4-mini-reasoning`, they received an error that the model wasn't found, which occurred because the download never completed so the model was never registered in Ollama's system.

Claude provided step-by-step guidance to clean up the partial download files, explaining that incomplete downloads are stored as partial blobs in the `~/.ollama/models/blobs/` directory. The user successfully located the partial files, which included a 3.1GB main partial file and multiple chunk tracker files all with the hash prefix "f4dd2368e6c3" and "-partial" suffix. Claude provided the exact command to remove only the phi4-related partial files while preserving other installed models from previous months. The cleanup successfully freed up approximately 2.5GB of disk space, reducing the total blob storage from about 9.5GB to 8.3GB. The user appears to have other Ollama models installed from June and November/December that remained intact after the cleanup.

chat_messages.text: i was downloading this how do i abort and uninstall? ollama run phi4-mini-reasoning

Ahab wants to abort an ongoing Ollama download and uninstall what's been downloaded. Let me give him the quick steps.**Abort the download:**
- Just hit `Ctrl+C` in the terminal to kill it

**Remove the model:**
```bash
ollama rm phi4-mini-reasoning
```

**If you want Ollama gone entirely:**
```bash
# Stop the service
sudo systemctl stop ollama

# Remove the binary
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/ollama

# Remove models and data
rm -rf ~/.ollama

# If installed via script, also remove the service
sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
```

Partial downloads should get cleaned up with the `ollama rm` command, but if anything's stuck, nuking `~/.ollama/models` handles it.

What made you bail on phi4-mini-reasoning?

Last login: Mon Dec  1 12:13:49 on ttys001
zayneamason@Zaynes-Air ~ % ollama run phi4-mini-reasoning
pulling manifest 
pulling f4dd2368e6c3:  41% ▕███████           ▏ 1.3 GB/3.2 GB   32 MB/s     56s^C
zayneamason@Zaynes-Air ~ % ollama rm phi4-mini-reasoning
Error: model 'phi4-mini-reasoning' not found
zayneamason@Zaynes-Air ~ %

The user aborted the download with Ctrl+C, but when they try to remove it, Ollama says the model isn't found. This makes sense - the download didn't complete, so the model was never actually registered.

The partial download files might still be sitting around though. Let me give them the quick fix.Makes sense — the model never finished pulling so it's not registered. The partial download chunks might still be hanging around though.

**Clean up partial downloads:**
```bash
# Check what's in the blobs folder (partial downloads live here)
ls -la ~/.ollama/models/blobs/

# Nuclear option - clear all incomplete downloads
rm -rf ~/.ollama/models/blobs/sha256-*-partial
```

Or if you want to be thorough and don't have other models you care about:
```bash
rm -rf ~/.ollama/models
```

You're good — nothing actually installed. 👍

chat_messages.text:
The user found the partial downloads. I can see the phi4-mini-reasoning partial files clearly - they're the ones with `f4dd2368e6c3` hash and `-partial` suffix. There's the main 3.1GB partial file plus a bunch of small chunk tracker files.

The other blobs are from other models the user has installed (dates go back to June and November/December), so I shouldn't tell them to nuke everything.There it is — that 3.1GB partial plus all those chunk trackers. Delete just the phi4 partials:

```bash
rm ~/.ollama/models/blobs/sha256-f4dd2368e6c32725dc1c5c5548ae9ee2724d6a79052952eb50b65e26288022c4-partial*
```

That'll free up ~3GB and leave your other models intact (looks like you've got a few from June/November/December still there).

The user successfully deleted the partial download files. The total went from 19805080 to 17261240 (in whatever units ls is showing - looks like 512-byte blocks on macOS), which is about 2.5GB freed up. The partial files are gone and only the complete model blobs remain.

I should confirm it's clean and maybe mention what models they still have installed if they want to know.Clean. Dropped from ~9.5GB to ~8.3GB in that folder — the phi4 partials are gone.

You've still got a few models installed (run `ollama list` if you want to see what's actually registered). Those big blobs you see are probably llama2 or similar from June/November.
